subject: FY2026 Budget Shortfall — urgent review needed
snippet: Ayumi — I've finished the updated projections. The picture is not great...
body:
Ayumi,

I've finished the updated projections for FY2026. The picture is not great. State per-pupil funding came in $340 below our working assumption, and the special-ed set-aside from the county is delayed until at least Q2. Combined, we're looking at a $2.1M structural shortfall against our adopted budget.

I've drafted three scenarios: (A) freeze all non-essential discretionary spending now, (B) draw down $900K of the stability reserve and freeze discretionary in Q3, or (C) a hybrid that preserves the Mirai STEM pilot but cuts the summer PD budget by 60%.

Can we find 30 minutes this week to walk through the numbers before the board study session? I'd like to have a recommendation framed before Patricia's office starts calling.

Marcus

subject: Re: FY2026 Budget Shortfall — urgent review needed
snippet: Marcus — thanks for the scenarios. Let's do Thursday at 3pm...
body:
Marcus,

Thank you for the quick turnaround on those projections. I've cc'd Grace so she can prep the instructional-impact lens before we meet.

Thursday at 3pm works for me. Please send a calendar hold to Angela and include the scenario doc in the invite so I can annotate beforehand.

My initial read: Scenario C is worth exploring, but I want to understand the ripple on classified staff hours before we commit to anything. Can you include a headcount sensitivity table in the deck?

Yumi

subject: Re: FY2026 Budget Shortfall — urgent review needed
snippet: Thursday 3pm confirmed. Headcount sensitivity table added...
body:
Thursday 3pm confirmed — Angela has the hold. I've added a headcount sensitivity table as Appendix B. The short version: Scenario C clips about 0.4 FTE equivalent in hourly classified time (mostly summer prep days), which is painful but recoverable. Scenario A is cleaner administratively but guts the family liaison stipends, which I know you won't want.

I'll have the full deck in your inbox by Wednesday noon.

Marcus

subject: Bell schedule proposal — CREA position
snippet: Dr. Meade — the Cascade Ridge Education Association has reviewed the proposed 7-period block schedule...
body:
Dr. Meade,

The Cascade Ridge Education Association has reviewed the proposed 7-period block schedule for the 2026–27 school year. Our members have serious concerns about the extended instructional periods and the loss of the daily prep block that teachers currently use for IEP compliance work.

Specifically:
1. The 90-minute block assumes high engagement throughout — research is mixed and our members report fatigue in classes with significant EL populations.
2. The daily prep period is contractually protected under Article 12, Section 4. Moving to an every-other-day model requires formal reopener negotiations.
3. We need a pilot evaluation plan with clear benchmarks before any full rollout.

We are open to a joint work group to examine alternatives. I'd like to schedule a meet-and-confer before the March 30 board deadline.

David Kowalski
President, CREA

subject: Re: Bell schedule proposal — CREA position
snippet: David — thank you for the detailed memo. I appreciate CREA's engagement...
body:
David,

Thank you for the detailed memo. I appreciate CREA's engagement and share the goal of protecting both instructional quality and working conditions.

I want to be direct: I take the Article 12 question seriously and have asked our labor counsel to review the prep-period provisions before we proceed. I do not want to inadvertently open a reopener through a board action.

On the substance, I think a joint work group is exactly the right vehicle. Grace will reach out this week to identify union reps for the group. I'm proposing three sessions in June with a recommendation to the board by the March 23 study session — tight but doable if we move quickly.

I'll personally attend the first session. Can you and I find 20 minutes by phone first to align on the ground rules?

Yumi

subject: Re: Bell schedule proposal — CREA position
snippet: Yumi — I appreciate the response. A call works. How about Tuesday 4pm?
body:
Yumi,

I appreciate the thoughtful response and the willingness to involve labor counsel proactively — that goes a long way with my members.

Tuesday at 4pm works for me. I'll call your direct line.

On the joint work group: I'd like to include our Special Ed chapter rep given the IEP prep-period issue. Will that work?

David

subject: Re: Bell schedule proposal — CREA position
snippet: Tuesday 4pm confirmed. Absolutely include the Spec Ed rep...
body:
Tuesday 4pm confirmed — I'll have my phone on.

Absolutely include the Special Ed chapter rep. That's the right instinct and will strengthen whatever recommendation we bring to the board.

Yumi

subject: Mirai STEM Grant — Cohort 2 scores are in
snippet: Dr. Meade — great news on the Mirai STEM grant evaluation...
body:
Dr. Meade,

Great news: the external evaluator submitted the Cohort 2 mid-year scores this morning. Average normalized gain on the CS/engineering design assessments is 0.62 SD across participating classrooms, which exceeds our Year 1 target of 0.45 SD. The disaggregated data also shows our EL students closing the gap from 0.38 SD to 0.55 SD — the strongest sub-group growth we've seen.

I've attached the full technical report (PDF) and a 2-page executive summary suitable for the board packet.

One ask: can we earmark a spot on the March 23 board agenda to present the preliminary findings? Even 8 minutes would be great for community visibility and positions us well for the Year 3 renewal application.

Tom Reilly
Mirai STEM Program Coordinator

subject: Re: Mirai STEM Grant — Cohort 2 scores are in
snippet: Tom — this is genuinely exciting. The EL sub-group data is exactly what we needed...
body:
Tom,

This is genuinely exciting — the EL sub-group growth is exactly the equity story we've been building toward. Thank you and the team for the rigorous tracking.

I'm cc'ing Angela to hold a spot on the March 23 agenda. Let's plan on 10 minutes to give you a little breathing room: 8 for the presentation and 2 for board questions.

Also — with the budget conversations we're having right now, this data is going to be important. Can you send me the one-page version by Monday? I want to have it in hand before Thursday's budget meeting with Marcus.

Yumi

subject: March 23 Board Meeting — packet deadline and agenda draft
snippet: Dr. Meade — attaching the draft agenda for the March 23 board meeting...
body:
Dr. Meade,

Attaching the draft agenda for the March 23 regular board meeting. A few items need your input before I finalize:

1. Action Item 4 (Revised LCAP Goals) — Legal flagged a citation error in Exhibit A. Do you want to pull it for July or present it with a verbal correction note?
2. Closed session: Patricia's office called to add a personnel item. I've blocked 30 minutes at the start. Does that work?
3. Superintendent report slot is currently 15 minutes. Do you want to expand it given the budget study item?

Packet deadline for the public-facing agenda is March 16 at noon. I need your sign-off by March 15 EOD.

Angela

subject: Re: March 23 Board Meeting — packet deadline and agenda draft
snippet: Angela — responses inline below...
body:
Angela,

Responses inline:

1. Pull the LCAP exhibit for July. A verbal correction at a public meeting creates a record issue — better to do it clean.
2. Thirty minutes for closed session is fine. Please confirm with Patricia's office that the item is properly noticed.
3. Yes, expand my report to 20 minutes. I want to frame the budget picture before the study item so the public hears context first.

I'll have the full sign-off to you by March 15 at 4pm.

Yumi

subject: Re: March 23 Board Meeting — packet deadline and agenda draft
snippet: Got it on all three. Patricia's office confirmed the closed session item is properly noticed...
body:
Got it on all three. Patricia's office confirmed the closed session item is properly noticed under Government Code 54957.

Updated draft will be in your inbox by end of day today for the March 15 sign-off. I'll also add Tom Reilly's Mirai STEM item (10 min) as you requested earlier.

Angela

subject: PAPEA Cookbook Fundraiser — superintendent recipe request
snippet: Hi Ayumi — hope summer is almost feeling real! We are deep into production on the PAPEA community cookbook...
body:
Hi Ayumi,

Hope summer is almost feeling real! We are deep into production on the PAPEA community cookbook and I would love — love — to include a recipe from you. It doesn't have to be fancy. A family weeknight staple, a holiday dish, something your bāchan taught you — whatever feels right.

Rosa is coordinating the submissions and can work with any format (handwritten scan, voice memo transcript, email). The deadline for final recipes is March 20 so we can hit the printer before July 4th weekend.

The cookbook will be sold at the back-to-school night in August and proceeds go directly to the family resource room supply fund. Last year we raised $4,200 — hoping to beat it this year.

Let me know if you have questions!
Michelle

subject: Re: PAPEA Cookbook Fundraiser — superintendent recipe request
snippet: Hi Dr. Meade — just adding a quick logistical note from my end...
body:
Hi Dr. Meade,

Just adding a quick logistical note: if you'd like to share a recipe in Japanese or include a note about its cultural context, we have a bilingual layout section specifically for that — it's been one of the most popular parts in past editions. Totally optional but wanted you to know the option exists.

You can email me directly at rosa.alvarez@papea.org or text the recipe to the submission line: (503) 555-0174. I'll handle formatting.

No pressure on the March 20 date — for the superintendent we can hold a slot until March 22 if needed!

Rosa

subject: Re: PAPEA Cookbook Fundraiser — superintendent recipe request
snippet: Michelle — I'm in. I'm going to submit my grandmother's chawanmushi recipe...
body:
Michelle,

I'm in! I'm going to submit my grandmother's chawanmushi recipe — it's the dish she always made when anyone in the family needed comfort. I'll write a short note about it.

I'll send it to Rosa by March 18 so there's no last-minute scramble. So glad the cookbook is happening again — the family resource room fund is one of my favorite things PAPEA does.

Yumi

subject: Mika's birthday party — venue shortlist
snippet: Hey — I've narrowed the venue options down to three for Mika's party...
body:
Hey,

I've narrowed it down to three options for Mika's birthday party. Quick rundown:

1. Cascade Pines Recreation Center — $180 for 3 hours, outdoor pavilion, can do the bounce house. They have October 19 open.
2. The Art Studio on Birch — $240 for 2 hours, ceramic painting party, max 14 kids. October 12 available.
3. Backyard with the Johnsons' pop-up tent (they offered) — free, but we'd need to rent tables and do all the food ourselves.

Mika keeps asking for 'the painting one' but I think she'll be happy with whatever. What's your gut?

B

subject: Re: Mika's birthday party — venue shortlist
snippet: The painting studio — 100%. She'll talk about it for years...
body:
The painting studio — 100%. She'll talk about it for years and the max-14-kids limit will save us from ourselves. October 12 works if it doesn't conflict with the district calendar (I'll check tomorrow).

Can you go ahead and hold it with a deposit? I'll Venmo you half.

Yumi

subject: End of year check-in + summer plans
snippet: Mom! Finals are done. I survived organic chem...
body:
Mom!

Finals are DONE. I survived organic chem (barely — do not ask about the last exam). I'm moving out of the dorm on March 14 and driving back with Priya, should be home by 8pm-ish.

Summer plan: I got the research assistant position in Professor Nakamura's lab (!!!) so I'll be on campus Mon/Wed/Fri starting March 22. But I'm free the other days and I was thinking maybe we could do a day trip to the coast before it gets crazy? Just us?

Also Bāchan keeps texting me asking if I'm eating enough. Can you reassure her I am?

Love you,
Hana

subject: Re: End of year check-in + summer plans
snippet: Hana!! The Nakamura lab — that's huge. I'm so proud of you...
body:
Hana!!

The Nakamura lab — that is HUGE. You've been wanting to work with her since sophomore year. I am so proud of you.

June 14 drive home sounds perfect. I'll make sure Bāchan knows you're coming and she'll probably try to have a full dinner ready by 6pm regardless of what time we tell her you'll arrive.

The coast day — yes, absolutely yes. Let's block the Saturday after you get back before either of our schedules fills up. I'll look at March 20.

And I will personally call Bāchan tonight and tell her you ate three full meals today. (Did you?)

So much love,
Mom

subject: Head Start volunteer reading schedule — summer session
snippet: Hi Ayumi — we're putting together the summer volunteer reading slots for Head Start...
body:
Hi Ayumi,

We're putting together the summer volunteer reading schedule for the Head Start program and I wanted to reach out to you personally before sending the general sign-up. Your visits last fall meant so much to the kids and staff — Mrs. Delgado still talks about how you stayed an extra half hour for questions.

Summer session runs October 7 through November 8, Tuesdays and Thursdays, 9–10am. We're asking volunteers for a minimum of two slots but of course any time you can give is wonderful.

No pressure at all given your schedule — even one visit this summer would be a gift. Let me know what works and I'll reserve a spot.

Warmly,
Karen

subject: Re: Head Start volunteer reading schedule — summer session
snippet: Karen — thank you for thinking of me. I would love to do two slots...
body:
Karen,

Thank you for thinking of me — those visits are genuinely some of my favorite mornings of the year.

Please put me down for two slots: October 10 (Thursday) and October 24 (Thursday), both 9–10am. If something opens up in August I'll let you know.

I'm planning to bring a picture book bilingual in Japanese and English if that would add anything for the group.

Yumi

subject: Equity audit community meeting — planning notes
snippet: Ayumi — I've drafted the planning notes for the equity audit community input meeting scheduled for October 15...
body:
Ayumi,

I've drafted the planning notes for the equity audit community input meeting scheduled for October 15. Key decisions needed from you:

1. Venue: I'm recommending Eastside Community Center (accessible, central to our highest-need attendance areas, parking). The district office conference room is an option but signals the wrong power dynamic for a community input process.
2. Facilitation: We could use an external facilitator from the county equity collaborative ($1,200 flat fee) or I can facilitate in-house. Given the sensitivity of some findings, I lean toward external.
3. Language access: I'm budgeting for Spanish and Somali interpretation. Do we need Tagalog coverage as well? The Eastgate enrollment data suggests yes.

I'd like to finalize the venue by March 12 to meet the public notice window.

Grace

subject: Re: Equity audit community meeting — planning notes
snippet: Grace — all three calls are exactly right. Let's go with Eastside...
body:
Grace,

All three calls are exactly right and I want to affirm them quickly so you can move.

1. Eastside Community Center — yes. You named the power dynamic issue precisely.
2. External facilitator — yes. Spend the $1,200. It's worth it for the credibility and it frees us to listen rather than manage process.
3. Add Tagalog. The Eastgate numbers are clear and we can't ask those families to navigate a meeting in a language that isn't theirs. Check with Karen Sato — she may know a community interpreter who works with Head Start.

Please book Eastside today. Thank you for driving this with so much care.

Yumi

subject: Re: Equity audit community meeting — planning notes
snippet: Eastside booked. External facilitator confirmed. Tagalog interpreter identified...
body:
Eastside is booked for October 15, 6–8pm. External facilitator confirmed — Dena Okafor from the county collaborative. She's facilitated two prior district equity sessions and is excellent.

On Tagalog: Karen Sato connected me with a Head Start community liaison who is a certified interpreter and is available October 15 at no cost to us. That's a real gift.

Spanish and Somali interpretation arrangements are in progress. Full logistics memo to follow by March 10.

Grace
